chunk_summary: This section addresses dyslipidemia management in pediatric obesity, focusing on lipid profile interpretation, urgent referral criteria, and statin therapy considerations within structured lifestyle interventions.
learning_objectives: Understand lipid profile thresholds for pediatric dyslipidemia interpretation.
Identify urgent referral criteria for high-risk lipid phenotypes in children.
Learn statin therapy indications and considerations for pediatric patients with obesity-related dyslipidemia.
key_facts: Total cholesterol thresholds: <170 mg/dL acceptable, 170–199 mg/dL borderline, ≥200 mg/dL high.
LDL thresholds: <110 mg/dL acceptable, 110–129 mg/dL borderline, ≥130 mg/dL high; urgent referral if LDL >190 mg/dL.
Triglycerides >500 mg/dL require urgent referral; fasting lipid panels are essential for accurate interpretation.
Statin therapy is considered for children ≥10 years with LDL ≥190 mg/dL or ≥160 mg/dL with additional risk factors after lifestyle interventions.
Dietary counseling should focus on reducing saturated fat, trans fat, refined carbohydrates, and sugary beverages.
reference_quote: NHLBI guidelines recommend urgent referral for LDL >190 mg/dL or triglycerides >500 mg/dL in pediatric patients with obesity-related dyslipidemia.
document_key_topics: Urgent referral for high-risk lipid phenotypes
Statin therapy in children with obesity
